decorators.name: @Controller
decorators.description: Defines a controller class that handles HTTP requests at a specific base route. All methods decorated with route decorators (@Get, @Post, etc.) will be relative to this path.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The base URL path for all routes in this controller (e.g., '/api/v1/users')

decorators.name: @Get
decorators.description: Decorates a method to handle HTTP GET requests. The path is relative to the controller's base path.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The route path for this endpoint. Defaults to '/' if not specified.

decorators.name: @Post
decorators.description: Decorates a method to handle HTTP POST requests. Typically used for creating new resources.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The route path for this endpoint. Defaults to '/' if not specified.

decorators.name: @Put
decorators.description: Decorates a method to handle HTTP PUT requests. Typically used for updating existing resources.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The route path for this endpoint. Defaults to '/' if not specified.

decorators.name: @Delete
decorators.description: Decorates a method to handle HTTP DELETE requests. Used for removing resources.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The route path for this endpoint. Defaults to '/' if not specified.

decorators.name: @Patch
decorators.description: Decorates a method to handle HTTP PATCH requests. Used for partial updates to resources.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The route path for this endpoint. Defaults to '/' if not specified.

decorators.name: @NoAuth
decorators.description: Marks an endpoint as public, bypassing authentication checks. Use this for endpoints that should be accessible without a valid token.

decorators.name: @HasUserType
decorators.description: Restricts endpoint access to users with specific user types. The authenticated user must have one of the specified types to access the endpoint.

parameters.name: userTypes
parameters.description: Array of allowed user types (e.g., ['ADMIN', 'MODERATOR'])

decorators.name: @HasGroups
decorators.description: Restricts endpoint access to users belonging to specific groups. The authenticated user must be a member of at least one of the specified groups.

parameters.name: groups
parameters.description: Array of group names that are allowed to access this endpoint

decorators.name: @RequiresOwnership
decorators.description: Validates that the authenticated user owns the requested resource. Compares the user ID from the token with a field in the request parameters.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Configuration object specifying how to verify ownership

parameters.name: options.paramName
parameters.description: The name of the request parameter containing the resource ID

parameters.name: options.userIdField
parameters.description: The field name in the user token containing the user's ID

decorators.name: @Schedule
decorators.description: Defines a scheduled task using cron expressions. The decorated class must extend ScheduledTask and implement the run() method.

parameters.name: cronExpression
parameters.description: Cron expression defining when the task runs (e.g., '0 0 * * *' for daily at midnight)

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Additional scheduling configuration

parameters.name: options.timezone
parameters.description: IANA timezone for the schedule (e.g., 'America/New_York')

parameters.name: options.runOnStart
parameters.description: Whether to run the task immediately when the application starts

decorators.name: @QueueWorker
decorators.description: Defines a queue worker that processes jobs from a BullMQ queue. The decorated class must implement IQueueWorkerHandler.

parameters.name: queueName
parameters.description: The name of the queue to consume jobs from

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Worker configuration options

parameters.name: options.concurrency
parameters.description: Number of jobs to process concurrently

decorators.name: @injectable
decorators.description: Marks a class as injectable for dependency injection. Required for any class that needs to be resolved from the DI container or have dependencies injected.

decorators.name: @inject
decorators.description: Injects a dependency into a class property or constructor parameter. Used with @injectable classes to resolve dependencies from the DI container.

parameters.name: serviceIdentifier
parameters.description: The identifier for the service to inject (typically from INVERSITY_TYPES)

classes.name: ScheduledTask
classes.description: Base class for scheduled tasks. Extend this class and decorate with @Schedule to create cron-based tasks. Provides lifecycle hooks for task execution.

classes.name: Containers
classes.description: Singleton class that manages the Inversify dependency injection container. Use getContainer() to access the DI container for binding and resolving services.

classes.name: PostgreSQLPersistenceContext
classes.description: PostgreSQL implementation of IPersistenceContext. Provides transaction management with automatic rollback on errors using TypeORM's EntityManager.

classes.name: APIError
classes.description: Custom error class for API responses. Includes HTTP status code and error code for structured error handling.

parameters.name: statusCode
parameters.description: HTTP status code (e.g., 400, 404, 500)

parameters.name: errorCode
parameters.description: Application-specific error code (e.g., 'NOT_FOUND', 'VALIDATION_ERROR')

parameters.name: message
parameters.description: Human-readable error message

classes.name: LoggerPino
classes.description: Pino-based logger factory implementation. Creates structured JSON loggers optimized for production environments.

parameters.name: serviceName
parameters.description: Name of the service for log identification

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Minimum log level (trace, debug, info, warn, error, fatal)

interfaces.name: IPersistenceContext
interfaces.description: Interface for transaction management. Implementations provide atomic database operations with automatic rollback on errors.

interfaces.name: IQueueClient
interfaces.description: Interface for queue job producers. Used in API services to add jobs to queues for async processing by workers.

interfaces.name: IQueueWorkerHandler
interfaces.description: Interface for queue job consumers. Implement this interface in classes decorated with @QueueWorker to process jobs.

interfaces.name: IDatabaseManagerFactory
interfaces.description: Interface for database connection management. Implementations handle connection pooling and DataSource creation for TypeORM.

interfaces.name: ILoggerFactory
interfaces.description: Interface for creating scoped loggers. Implementations provide structured logging with service and component context.

functions.name: createApplication
functions.description: Creates and configures a MangoJS application instance. This is the main entry point for bootstrapping your microservice.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Application configuration options

parameters.name: options.controllers
parameters.description: Array of controller classes to register

parameters.name: options.middleware
parameters.description: Global middleware to apply to all routes

parameters.name: options.container
parameters.description: Custom Inversify container (uses default if not provided)

types.name: INVERSITY_TYPES
types.description: Symbols used as injection tokens for the dependency injection container. Use these with @inject() to resolve services.

types.name: AuthUserType
types.description: Enumeration of user types for authentication and authorization. Used with @HasUserType decorator to restrict endpoint access.

types.name: ScheduleOptions
types.signature: type ScheduleOptions = { timezone?: string; runOnStart?: boolean }

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: IANA timezone identifier (e.g., 'America/New_York', 'Europe/London')

parameters.name: runOnStart
parameters.description: Execute the task immediately when the application starts

types.name: QueueWorkerOptions

parameters.name: concurrency
parameters.description: Maximum number of jobs to process simultaneously

errors.name: APIError
errors.description: Standard error class for API responses. Thrown from services and automatically handled by the error middleware to return consistent JSON error responses.

parameters.name: statusCode
parameters.description: HTTP status code for the response

parameters.name: errorCode
parameters.description: Machine-readable error code

parameters.name: message
parameters.description: Human-readable error message
